Subject: Din kostnadsfrie prøveperiode på Prime Video har startet
Body:
========
Amazon Prime Video
https://www.primevideo.com
========
Hei,
Gratulerer! Den kostnadsfrie prøveperioden på Prime Video har startet. Med Prime Video kan du nå gjøre følgende:
- Se populære filmer og TV-serier, blant annet Amazon Original-serier som The Grand Tour, American Gods og The Man in the High Castle. [ https://primevideo.com ]
- Se hvor som helst og når som helst når du laster ned Prime Video-appen til mobile enheter eller utvalgte Smart-TV-er.
- I tillegg får du tilgang til Twitch Prime (twitch.amazon.com/prime). Få kostnadsfrie spill, «loot» i spill og mye mer på Twitch.tv – verdens ledende sosiale videosamfunn for gamere.

Da du registrerte deg, bekreftet du at du hadde lest og godtatt Prime Video bruksvilkårene [https://www.primevideo.com/tou], og du godkjente at vi belaster den angitte betalingsmetoden eller en annen tilgjengelig lagret betalingsmetode etter den 7 dager lange kostnadsfrie prøveperioden. Prime Video-medlemskapet ditt fornyes automatisk til det avsluttes for 79,00 kr/måned (se mer informasjon om gjeldende avgifter [https://www.primevideo.com/taxInfoUrl]). Du kan avslutte medlemskapet når som helst ved å gå til Min konto [ https://www.primevideo.com/settings
] og endre medlemskapsinnstillingene.

Takk,
Amazon Prime Video

Merk: Denne e-postmeldingen ble sendt til your email @gmail.com fra en ubetjent e-postadresse som ikke kan motta innkommende meldinger. Ikke svar på denne meldingen.

Subject: [UAI] 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems
Body: FINAL Call for papers - 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications (AISTA2004) **** Please apologize for multiple copies **** FINAL Call for Papers AISTA 2004 --> 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications November 15-18, 2004 --> Centre de Recherche Public Henri Tudor, Luxembourg-Kirchberg, Luxembourg http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004 IMPORTANT DATES: Submission of extended summaries: September 15, 2004 Notification of acceptance: September 26, 2004 Camera-ready paper due: October 24, 2004 AISTA Conference: November 15-18, 2004 AIMS and SCOPE: The International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications - AISTA'2004 aims at bringing together theoreticians and practitioners of Intelligent Systems from Luxembourg and around the world to exchange ideas, present their recent results and discuss possible collaborations. The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. PROGRAM CHAIRS * Dr. Eng. Djamel KHADRAOUI - CRP Henri Tudor (Luxembourg) - nzjwoo.gluzshfek@tudor.lu * Dipl. Ing. (FH) Frank ZIMMER - SES ASTRA (Luxembourg) - xfdnz.tlqykm@ses-astra.com * Professor Pascal BOUVRY - University of Luxembourg -=20 sexlxi.qnlpjj@uni.lu * Dr. Masoud Mohammadian Senior Lecturer - University of Canberra (Australia) - ismgpv@ise.canberra.edu.au INTERNATIONAL PROGRAM COMMITTE Masoud Mohammadian (University of Canberra, Australia) Jorge Barboza (None, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil) Bruno Clemente Guingo (Funda=E7=E3o Educacional Serra dos =D3rg=E3os, Teres= =F3polis, Brazil) Rachida Dssouli (Concordia University, Canada) Janos D. Pinter (Dalhousie University, Canada) Jianmin Sun (bicea, Beijing, China) Mario Zagar (University of Croatia, Croatia) Jarmo Alander (University of Vaasa, Finland) Juha Karhunen (Helsinki University of Technology, Finland) Jacques Blanc-Talon (CTA-GIP, France) Olivier Boissier (Ecole des Mines Saint-Etienne, France) Isabelle Chrisment (Loria-Inria Lorraine, France) Yves Demazeau (Institut IMAG - Grenoble, France) Talbi El-ghazali (Lille University, France) Francine Herrmann (Metz University, France) Yves Ducq (Bordeaux University, France) Zahia Guessoum (LIP6, Paris 6, France) Jacques Calmet (University of Karlsruhe, Germany) Ralf Denzer (University of Applied Sciences, Saarbr=FCcken, Germany) Reiner G=FCttler (University of Applied Sciences, Saarbr=FCcken, Germany) Axel Poign=E9 (Fraunhofer Institut Autonome, Intelligente Systeme, Germany) Bernd Reusch (University of Dortmund, Germany) Christoph Meinel (University at Trier, Germany) Shun-ichi Amari (The Institute of Physical, and Chimical Research (RIKEN), Japan) Takesshi Furuhashi (Nagoya University, Japan) Sugisaka Masanori (Oita University, Japan) Ning Zhong (Yamaguchi University, Japan) Pascal Bouvry (University of Luxembourg,Luxembourg) Eric Dubois (CRP Henri Tudor, Luxembourg) Benjamin Gateau (LIASIT, Luxembourg - Gasperich, Luxembourg) Serge Gille (CRP Henri Tudor, Luxembourg) Nicolas Guelfi (University of Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Farid Meinkohn (Cybercultus - Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Frank Zimmer (SES ASTRA, Luxembourg, Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Djamel Khadraoui (CRP Henri Tudor, Luxembourg) Christoph Schommer (University of Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Emil Weydert (University of Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Steffen Rothkugel (University of Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Thomas Engel (University of Luxembourg, Luxembourg) Thibaud Latour (CRP henri Tudor, Luxembourg) Joost N. text:
[UAI] 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems

FINAL Call for papers - 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications (AISTA2004) **** Please apologize for multiple copies **** FINAL Call for Papers AISTA 2004 --> 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications November 15-18, 2004 --> Centre de Recherche Public Henri Tudor, Luxembourg-Kirchberg, Luxembourg http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004 IMPORTANT DATES: Submission of extended summaries: September 15, 2004 Notification of acceptance: September 26, 2004 Camera-ready paper due: October 24, 2004 AISTA Conference: November 15-18, 2004 AIMS and SCOPE: The International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications - AISTA'2004 aims at bringing together theoreticians and practitioners of Intelligent Systems from Luxembourg and around the world to exchange ideas, present their recent results and discuss possible collaborations. The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. Subject: AI’s 'Printing Press Moment'
Body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: Billionaire’s Deal Of A LifetimeADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER • Newsletters • MyForbesGood morning, It’s about time. For AI leaders like OpenAI CEO Sam Altman to call for greater regulation. For “productive and direct” meetings to raise the debt ceiling. A Golden Bachelor starring seniors looking for love.There's much more. Enjoy, Caroline Howard Executive Editor Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSGETTY IMAGESPresident Joe Biden and House Speaker Kevin McCarthy emerged with a positive outlook following their latest round of debt ceiling negotiations. McCarthy said a deal to raise the nation’s borrowing limit could be done “by the end of the week.” The two sides appear hung up on a handful of details ahead of an estimated June 1 deadline.BUSINESS + FINANCESwiss banking giant UBS said it was pressured into buying its ailing rival Credit Suisse for much more than it initially bargained for, warning investors it stands to lose billions in legal and regulatory costs from the rushed deal and illuminating the backstage workings that drove one of the banking world’s most consequential deals in decades.Investor sentiment tanked to its lowest level of 2023 this month, according to a Bank of America survey of large fund managers, revealing the lingering effects of a stalling stock market amid a sour macroeconomic backdrop—and worries over a potential debt ceiling impasse in Washington.TECH & INNOVATIONASSOCIATED PRESSOpenAI CEO Sam Altman and other leaders of the burgeoning AI industry voiced support for additional regulation while testifying at a historic congressional hearing—in contrast to some powerful tech firms that have pushed back against regulatory intervention. Altman called the technology a “printing press moment” that requires federal oversight.The Justice Department charged a former Apple employee with stealing information about the company’s autonomous vehicle technology before leaving for a China-based competitor, one of five cases involving illegally diverting U.S. technology and materials. Two cases accuse former software engineers of stealing materials to market to Chinese competitors, while two others centered on helping Russian military and intelligence services.WEALTH + ENTREPRENEURSHIPThe revelation that billionaire George Soros' investment management firm sold off its entire Tesla stake last quarter seemingly kicked off an otherwise unprompted "dangerous" attack from Elon Musk. But the firm is one of many that sold off loads of Tesla shares last quarter, cashing in on the stock's nearly 70% rise following its dismal 2022 performance, according to regulatory filings.SCIENCE + HEALTHCAREASSOCIATED PRESSThe Federal Trade Commission filed a lawsuit to block biotech giant Amgen from purchasing Horizon Therapeutics for $27.8 billion out of concerns the merger would harm patients on Horizon’s two fastest-growing drugs—Tepezza, which treats thyroid eye disease, and gout therapy Krystexxa. With a combined some $2.7 billion in sales last year, neither drug has any competition.Munjal Shah and his Hippocratic AI cofounders are ready to solve the looming healthcare staffing shortage. The Palo Alto-based has raised a $50 million seed round to develop a large language model that will power healthcare bots to provide guidance on nutrition, genetics counseling, insurance billing and more–with one major caveat: they won’t diagnose medical conditions—yet.SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTABC will launch The Golden Bachelor—a new addition to The Bachelor franchise in which a senior man will date a house full of senior women—this fall, as the network pushes to fill more time slots with reality television amid a writers’ strike with no end in sight.TRAVEL & LIFESTYLEBeauty brands using technology is nothing new. But traditionally, they tap tech to improve and enhance products. New brand KIKI is taking a different route by using tech to change the consumer experience. It uses blockchain technology to make consumers co-creators by giving them a voice in every product launch.Daily Cover Story JAMEL TOPPIN FOR FORBESBillionaire’s Deal Of A Lifetime: Can David Adelman Build An NBA Arena In Center City Philadelphia?READ ARTICLETOPLINEAs the public face of a huge development that’s not popular with everyone, David Adelman, CEO of Campus Apartments, has gone all-in on getting a new home for the 76ers—and gotten hammered for it.His career had been fabulous but not flashy enough to attract the heat of the media spotlight. He was no tech wizard with a viral new platform. In 2007, he helped create what’s now called FS Investments; today it manages $35 billion. And he founded the Darco Capital family office, where he sprinkled his money on startups and established businesses that reflect his bedrock belief in his hometown.That changed last year when Adelman, a minority stakeholder in the Philadelphia 76ers, became the face of the NBA team’s effort to build a $1.3 billion arena called 76 Place in Philly’s Center City. Adelman has finally emerged from relative obscurity because the plan calls for demolishing a part of a huge mall that sits on top of the Jefferson Street rail station, where it could create havoc for hundreds of residents and businesses.WHY IT MATTERSTo hear Adelman tell it, 76 Place will add $1.9 billion and 9,000 jobs to the local economy during construction and $400 million annually and 1,000 permanent jobs after it opens. Local groups say it would destroy the community by causing prices to skyrocket for Chinatown residents, bringing swarms on game nights and making parking impossible.MOREThe NFL Says It Spent $125 Million To Boost Black-Owned Businesses. Where Did The Money Go?ADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSSupport for childhood vaccination remains high after the Covid-19 pandemic, despite shifting attitudes toward school mandates and persistent skepticism over coronavirus shots, according to a new Pew Research Center poll. A small dose, but there’s more. 9 in 10Americans say the benefits of childhood vaccines for measles, mumps and rubella outweigh the risks 7 in 10Believe children should be vaccinated to attend public school, down from 82% in 2016 and 2019 28%Support parents’ right to choose whether to vaccinate their children, up 12 points from 2019STRATEGY AND SUCCESSGiving your salary requirements on a job application or interview is always awkward. Ask for too much, and you might be rejected. Too little, and you’re selling yourself short. A best practice is to request 20% more than you want, so there’s room to negotiate. Here are other strategies to prepare for the inevitable money question. VIDEOThe Key Principles To Unlocking Financial Freedom Through Crypto And Web3Watch nowGAMESQUIZBillionaire investor Charles Johnson took the owners of a proposed eatery to court to invalidate its liquor license. It's the latest development in an ongoing battle in which ultra-wealthy second-home enclave?A.AspenB.East HamptonC.Jackson HoleD.NantucketCheck Your AnswerFRASE BY FORBESUnscramble today’s anagram to solve this hidden phrase!Scroll to the bottom of today’s newsletter for a hint.Play Now TODAY'S PHRASE ACROSS THE NEWSROOMAnnouncing the 2023 Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Lists. We’re looking for the top young founders and leaders in the innovation hubs of Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Los Angeles, Miami, Puerto Rico, Seattle and Toronto. Nominations are now open.The Forbes third annual 50 Over 50 is officially looking for its next class of women proving that success has no age limit. We’re looking for women stepping into their own after age 50. We’ll explore the forthcoming impact of artificial intelligence, shifting dynamics between workers and employers, and more. Sign up to stay tuned for updates.Thanks for reading! Subject: Re: [opensuse] Unstable system - who is the culprit?
Body: On Saturday 09 February 2008 10:08, Philippe Landau wrote: > ... > > But many power supplies are only efficient when > they don't operate close to full load. This seemed counterintuitive to me, so I decided to do a little looking around. According to the graph on page 14 of "Power Supplies: A Hidden Opportunity for Energy Savings" this is not the case. Switching power-supplies have an efficiency peak when producing about 50% of their maximum rated output. However the curve on the low side of the efficiency peak is much steeper than that on the high side. So my suspicion was mostly correct. A PSU that has more than twice the capacity that it will be called on to supply will waste significantly more power than one being used in the 50% to 100% range. > kind regards Philippe Randall Schulz
text:

On Saturday 09 February 2008 10:08, Philippe Landau wrote: > ... > > But many power supplies are only efficient when > they don't operate close to full load. Switching power-supplies have an efficiency peak when producing about 50% of their maximum rated output. However the curve on the low side of the efficiency peak is much steeper than that on the high side. So my suspicion was mostly correct. A PSU that has more than twice the capacity that it will be called on to supply will waste significantly more power than one being used in the 50% to 100% range. > kind regards Philippe Randall Schulz

Subject: Re: Strange headers on mail from SA users list
Body: >>I'd really like this to be fixed as its littering my mail archiving >>system with junk addresses. I've attached the complete, unmodified >>message as "annoyance'txt". > The message received at your end shows up as > "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit" whereas the original message is > "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit". > The transfer-encoding conversion may have caused the header problem. That sounds likely. Thanks for the information. I already have a filter installed immediately downstream of SA, so this is easy to check by extending it: any header byte containing 0x80 or higher is invalid because headers must be US-ASCII encoding (RFC 2821). I can log the details of any messages containing them. As for detoxifying the headers and stopping any further upsets, at a guess something like: if (c > 0x7f) { c = c & 0x80; if (c < 0x20) c = c + 'A"; } would detoxify the invalid header bytes headers without adding significant problems. Comments? Martin

As for detoxifying the headers and stopping any further upsets, at a guess something like: if (c > 0x7f) { c = c & 0x80; if (c < 0x20) c = c + 'A"; } would detoxify the invalid header bytes headers without adding significant problems. Subject: Re: [ILUG] Sun Solaris..
Body: On Thu, Aug 22, 2002 at 05:13:01PM +0100, Fergal Moran mentioned: > In a nutshell - Solaris is Suns own flavour of UNIX. Though I'm sure that this nice person would like a bit more detail. Solaris is quite different to Linux, though these days you can make solaris act a lot like linux with an extra CD of GNU tools Sun ship with solaris. It is based on the SysV unix family, so it's quite similar to other unixen like HPUX and SCO. Sun's hardware in general is more reliable, and a lot more expensive. One of the main bonuses you get by buying Sun is that you are getting your hardware and software from one company, so if you have a support contract, they have to fix it. They can't fob you off with 'that's a software problem, talk to the software vendor.' etc. If you are set on Linux, you most likely can do your own support. There is then a world of different hardware options. You can run Linux on Sparc, though some companies like RedHat don't maintain a sparc port anymore. You can also buy your machine from linux-oriented companies like DNUK, who do machines designed to run linux, and their own version of linux, that has a few extras for their machines. Or, you can get a machine from a cheaper company like Dell, and it'll most likely work, most of the time. John -- Irish Linux Users' Group: ilug@linux.ie http://www.linux.ie/mailman/listinfo/ilug for (un)subscription information. List maintainer: listmaster@linux.ie

Subject: Re: [SM-USERS] Bug Report
Body: On Thu, February 7, 2008 17:30, Gabrielle wrote: > I subscribe to the squirrelmail-users mailing list. > [x] True - No need to CC me when replying > [ ] False - Please CC me when replying > > This bug occurs when I ... > ... view a particular message > ... use a specific plugin/function > ... try to do/view/use .... > > > > The description of the bug: > Each + EVERY TIME I login to my account: < aquagabe >, I am Besieged by > SPAM MAIL (A large # are of crude, sexual nature which I find VERY > OFFENSIVE). I have been 'Literally' Over-run by SPAM MAIL for Many > Years in this account. Nothing and it seems NOBODY @ VCN's > Squirrelmail has taken the time to help me, Resolve this. I ended up > coming in and opening two other accounts - just so I could Email my > friends (whom I 'want' to hear from, without having to deal with all this > hassel), and the other was for Correspondence School Work Studies. -Now > that One is also being besieged by SPAM MAIL. --Can't Anything Be Done > About This? > --Can I Close This Account Completely and ReFresh or ReOpen it ANEW??? > Has your ISP installed, configured SPAMASSIN? You might look at Options->MailFilters as a work around. We host mailboxes for the clients who have websites with us and we've combined SPAMASSIN and a home grown prefilter which does something similar to the mailfilter function, but in front of spamassin. Has cut down my spam to about 1/6 original volume and mailfilter will move the offensive ones that get by out as well. One thing, don't delete the spam because you will have false positives (i.e., ham that gets marked as spam). Setting up spamassin is an ISP IPP function. so you need to talk to them. Use the mailfilter until this happens and as a backup afterwards. Just my $0.02 Bill

I ended up > coming in and opening two other accounts - just so I could Email my > friends (whom I 'want' to hear from, without having to deal with all this > hassel), and the other was for Correspondence School Work Studies. -Now > that One is also being besieged by SPAM MAIL. --Can't Anything Be Done > About This? > --Can I Close This Account Completely and ReFresh or ReOpen it ANEW??? > Has your ISP installed, configured SPAMASSIN? You might look at Options->MailFilters as a work around. We host mailboxes for the clients who have websites with us and we've combined SPAMASSIN and a home grown prefilter which does something similar to the mailfilter function, but in front of spamassin. Has cut down my spam to about 1/6 original volume and mailfilter will move the offensive ones that get by out as well. One thing, don't delete the spam because you will have false positives (i.e., ham that gets marked as spam). Setting up spamassin is an ISP IPP function. so you need to talk to them. Use the mailfilter until this happens and as a backup afterwards. Just my $0.02 Bill

Subject: Viktig info om oppdateringen av visningsnavn på FINN
Body: Hei! Tidligere denne uken mottok du en e-post fra oss i FINN der vi informerte om at vi har endret visningsnavnet på din FINN-profil. Det gikk dessverre litt fort i svingene da vi gjorde denne endringen, og vi har derfor endret visningsnavnet ditt tilbake til slik det var før. Som vi nevnte i vår første epost, ble endringene av visningsnavn gjort som et ekstra sikkerhetstiltak. Den siste tiden har vi sett en del tilfeller der folk med uærlige hensikter har prøvd å gjette de private e-postadressene til FINN-brukere, basert på visningsnavnet deres på FINN. Etter å ha gjennomført endringen, ser vi imidlertid at det ikke var noen større sammenheng mellom visningsnavnet ditt og mailadressen vi har registrert på deg, og derfor har vi endret visningsnavnet ditt tilbake - beklager bryderiet! Vi oppfordrer fortsatt alle våre brukere til å ha et bevisst forhold til visningsnavnet sitt, og være klar over at dette kan brukes for å prøve å lure deg hvis det er veldig likt din virkelige identitet/navn. Dersom du ser at visningsnavnet ditt på FINN enkelt kan kobles til en av dine e-postadresser, oppfordrer vi deg til å endre visningsnavnet. Det kan du enkelt gjøre på Min FINN - Min konto - trykk på blyanten ved “Om deg”. Beklager om vi har skapt litt forvirring med dette, vi ønsker å hjelpe våre brukere til å ha det trygt når de bruker FINN. Ta gjerne kontakt med oss hvis du har spørsmål – vi er her for å hjelpe! Hilsen oss i FINN
